paras.text: Agle din, {{A}} ka phone baja — ye {{B}} ki roommate thi, kisi wajah se pareshan lag rahi thi.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (phone pe, thodi confuse) Yaar, meri roommate ne kal mujhse kaha 'kya tum mera laptop use kar sakti ho' — maine use bola 'nahi mujhe zarurat nahi', but aaj wo naraz hai, bol rahi hai maine uske baare me kuch aur bola tha kisi aur ko!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Lगता hai koi galat 'report' hui hai beech me — jaise Chinese whispers! Chalo, ye ek achha mauka hai — humne pichli baar sirf STATEMENTS ka indirect speech seekha tha, ab baaki sab types bhi seekh lete hain, taaki aisi galatfehmi kabhi na ho tumhare saath.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Haan please, ye bahut zaroori lag raha hai ab. Pehle QUESTIONS wala batao — jaisa maine abhi bola, 'kya tum laptop use kar sakti ho' — usko report kaise karte hain?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Questions do type ke hote hain — YES/NO questions (jinka jawab sirf haan/na me ho sakta hai), aur WH-questions (what/where/when/why/how wale). Dono ka rule thoda alag hai. Pehle YES/NO wala dekhte hain.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo bolo.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: She asked (me) if/whether I could use her laptop. — dekho TEEN cheezein badli: (1) 'said' ki jagah 'asked' aaya, (2) 'if' ya 'whether' use kiya joड़ne ke liye, (3) question mark hata diya aur word order NORMAL statement jaisa ban gaya — 'could I use' nahi, 'I could use'.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (hairan) Ohh, matlab jo galti meri roommate ki baat report karne me hui, wahi thi — maine shayad word order galat rakha ya 'if/whether' nahi use kiya, isliye matlab hi badal gaya!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Bilkul ho sakta hai! Ye bahut common mistake hai. Chalo ab WH-questions dekhte hain, jo thoda alag hote hain.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Haan bolo, fark kya hai?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: He asked me where I was going. — dekho, yahan 'if/whether' ki zarurat nahi padi, kyunki 'where' khud hi connector ban gaya. Baaki rule same hai — 'said' 'asked' banta hai, word order normal statement jaisa, question mark hata do.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (notebook me likhte hue) Achha, matlab YES/NO me 'if/whether' lagta hai, WH-questions me khud question-word hi kaafi hai. Word order dono me normal ho jaata hai. Ek practice do na.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Chalo — 'She said, "What time does the class start?"' Isko convert karo.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (sochte hue) 'said' 'asked' banega, 'what time' wahi rahega connector ke roop me, word order normal — 'She asked what time the class started.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Perfect! Ab agla type — IMPERATIVE sentences, matlab commands, requests, advice. Jaise jab koi kisi ko order deta hai ya request karta hai.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Achha, jaise 'Close the door' jaisa sentence?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ekdum, chalo dekhte hain iska rule — The teacher told me to close the door. — dekho, poora verb 'TO + base form' ban gaya ('to close'), aur reporting verb context ke hisaab se badla — order ho to 'ordered/told', request ho to 'requested', advice ho to 'advised'.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Achha, kaise pata chalega ki reporting verb 'ordered' use karna hai ya 'requested'?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Tone/context dekhna padta hai. Agar 'please' ho ya politely bola gaya ho, to 'requested'. Agar koi strict order/authority wali baat ho (jaise teacher, boss, police), to 'ordered' ya 'told'. Agar suggestion/advice ho, to 'advised'.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Achha, aur agar NEGATIVE command ho, jaise 'mat karo'?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Bahut achha sawaal! Negative imperative me 'NOT TO + V1' use hota hai. Jaise 'She said, "Don't touch that."' Indirect me — 'She told me not to touch that.' Bas 'not' 'to' se pehle lagana hai.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (pakka likhte hue) Achha, ab practice karte hain isme bhi — 'He said, "Please don't be late."'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ye tum khud try karo, main sirf hint dunga — 'please' hai, matlab request, aur 'don't' hai, matlab negative.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (sochte hue) To hoga — 'He requested me not to be late.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Perfect! Ab do aur types bache hain, thode kam common but exam me aa sakte hain — EXCLAMATORY aur OPTATIVE sentences.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Exclamatory matlab 'Wow!' jaisi baatein? Aur Optative?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Bilkul, Exclamatory matlab joy/surprise/sorrow dikhane wale sentences — 'What a beautiful place!' jaise. Optative matlab wish/prayer wale sentences — 'May you succeed!' jaise. Chalo Exclamatory pehle dekhte hain.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: She exclaimed with joy that it was a beautiful place. — dekho, 'said' ki jagah 'exclaimed with joy/sorrow/surprise' (feeling ke hisaab se) aaya, aur 'What a beautiful place!' normal statement ban gaya — 'it was a (very) beautiful place', exclamation mark bhi hata diya.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Achha, kaise pata chalega feeling kya thi — joy, sorrow, ya surprise?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Context aur words se pata chalta hai — agar 'Alas!' ya koi sad baat hai, to 'sorrow'. Agar 'Hurray!' ya khushi wali baat hai, to 'joy'. Agar bahut shocking/unexpected baat hai, to 'surprise' ya 'wonder'.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Achha ab last wala, Optative — wish/prayer?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: She prayed that I might succeed in life. — dekho, 'said' 'prayed' ya 'wished' ban gaya, aur 'may' 'might' ban gaya, baaki normal statement jaisa structure.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (poora chart bana ke) Chalo ab main poora summary banata hoon — Statement: tense/pronoun/time-place shift, 'said' wahi rehta hai. Yes/No Question: 'asked' + if/whether + normal word order. Wh-Question: 'asked' + question-word + normal word order. Imperative: 'told/ordered/requested/advised' + to+V1 (ya not to+V1 for negative). Exclamatory: 'exclaimed with joy/sorrow' + normal statement, exclamation hata do. Optative: 'wished/prayed' + 'might' (may ki jagah).

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Wah, bilkul perfect summary! Ab chalo poora practice karte hain, sab types mix karke — main paanch sentences dunga.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo, ready hoon!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Pehla — 'She said, "Do you have my book?"' (Yes/No Question)

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: 'She asked if/whether I had her book.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Sahi! Doosra — 'The doctor said, "Take rest for two days."' (Imperative — advice)

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: 'The doctor advised me to take rest for two days.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Perfect! Teesra — 'He said, "Alas! I have lost my job."' (Exclamatory — sorrow)

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: 'He exclaimed with sorrow that he had lost his job.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ekdum sahi! Chautha — 'She said, "Why are you crying?"' (Wh-Question)

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: 'She asked me why I was crying.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Perfect! Aakhri — 'The old man said, "May you be happy always!"' (Optative)

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: 'The old man prayed/wished that I might be happy always.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Full marks! Dekho, ab tum poori Indirect Speech ki master ban gayi ho — statements, questions, commands, exclamations, wishes, sab kuch.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (khush hoke) Thank you! Ab main apni roommate ko sahi se poora explain kar sakti hoon ki galatfehmi kaise hui. Waise iske liye bhi tumhara shukriya karna chahiye — should I say it in indirect speech?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hasm ke) Try karo!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (muskurate hue) 'I told him that he was the best teacher I could have asked for.' Dekha, maine khud bana liya, bina soche!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (thoda sharma ke) Perfect sentence... aur perfect student.

quiz.q: Convert to Indirect Speech: She said, "Are you feeling well?"
quiz.answer: She asked if/whether I was feeling well.
quiz.explain: Yes/No question — 'said' becomes 'asked', 'if/whether' joड़ता hai, word order normal statement jaisa ho jaata hai (question mark hata do).

quiz.q: Convert to Indirect Speech: He said, "Where do you live?"
quiz.explain: Wh-question — question-word ('where') khud hi connector hai, 'if/whether' ki zarurat nahi. Word order normal statement jaisa.

quiz.q: Convert to Indirect Speech: The teacher said, "Submit your assignments by Friday."
quiz.answer: The teacher told/ordered us to submit our assignments by Friday.
quiz.explain: Imperative (command) — poora verb 'to + V1' ban jaata hai, reporting verb 'told' ya 'ordered' (authority context).

quiz.q: Convert to Indirect Speech: She said, "Please don't disturb me."
quiz.answer: She requested me not to disturb her.
quiz.explain: Negative imperative + request — 'not to + V1' use hota hai, aur 'please' ki wajah se reporting verb 'requested' hai.

quiz.q: Convert to Indirect Speech: He said, "Hurray! We have won the match."
quiz.answer: He exclaimed with joy that they had won the match.
quiz.explain: Exclamatory (joy) — 'said' 'exclaimed with joy' banta hai, exclamation hata ke normal statement, tense bhi backshift hoti hai (have won → had won).

quiz.q: Convert to Indirect Speech: The mother said, "May you have a long and happy life!"
quiz.answer: The mother prayed/wished that I/he/she might have a long and happy life.
quiz.explain: Optative (prayer/wish) — 'said' 'prayed/wished' banta hai, 'may' 'might' ban jaata hai.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'She asked that did I have her book.'
quiz.answer: She asked if/whether I had her book.
quiz.explain: Yes/No question me 'that' nahi, 'if/whether' use hota hai, aur word order normal statement jaisa hona chahiye ('did I have' galat, 'I had' sahi).

quiz.q: Mixed — Which reporting verb fits: request, order, or advice? 'You should drink more water.'
quiz.answer: Advice — 'He advised me to drink more water.'
quiz.explain: 'Should' suggestion/advice dikhata hai, isliye reporting verb 'advised' fit hota hai, 'ordered' ya 'requested' nahi.
